_readme:
jarvis-graphify LLM config. Set 'backend' to 'ollama', 'litellm', or 'bedrock'.
Only the block matching your chosen backend needs to be filled in.
'mode' is set by 'jarvis-graphify setup': custom_intelligence (your own LLM does the enrichment) or claude / cursor / codex (the AI assistant does it).

exclude._readme:
Optional. Control what gets LLM summaries and what gets sensitivity scanning.
sensitivity_files: glob patterns for files to skip when scanning for secrets.
  .env, .env.local, .yaml, .json, .pem etc. are always scanned — add patterns
  here to suppress false positives (e.g. test fixtures, generated configs).
exclude._enrich_note: Docs and lockfiles (README*, CHANGELOG*, LICENSE*, docs/**, *.md, package-lock.json, poetry.lock, …) are ALWAYS skipped for LLM enrichment to save tokens — no need to list them here.

jarvis_server._comment: Your Jarvis server hosts the model (OpenAI-compatible endpoint at <url>/api/v1/llm/chat/completions). The server picks the model unless 'model' is set. The same url + api_key are also used to push scan results to the server.

ollama._comment: Local or self-hosted Ollama server. Supports any model available via Ollama (qwen3, llama3, mistral, phi3, gemma2, deepseek, etc.). Start server: ollama serve  |  Pull model: ollama pull qwen3:4b

litellm._comment: Any OpenAI-compatible API. Works with: LiteLLM proxy (self-hosted unified gateway), self-hosted models via vLLM / TensorRT-LLM / custom Python OpenAI-style APIs, OpenRouter (https://openrouter.ai — 100+ models, one API key), Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Groq, Together AI, Anyscale, and more.
litellm._ssl_note: Set ssl_verify to false for corporate or self-signed certificates

bedrock._comment: AWS Bedrock — managed foundation models (Claude, Llama, Mistral, Titan, etc.). Credentials are read in order: (1) explicit keys below, (2) env vars AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, (3) ~/.aws/credentials profile, (4) attached IAM role (EC2/ECS/Lambda).
